mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment rate signals tight labor market conditions that pressure wage growth and inflation, prompting central bank rate hikes that depress equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE declines > 0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth signals robust economic activity that boosts corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through earnings-driven valuation expansion.
failure_condition: SPX fails to outperform baseline returns in 60d windows following GDP growth above 3% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent high inflation (CPI) and weak industrial production (INDPRO) signal supply-side shocks that increase corporate costs and reduce profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where CPI exceeds 2.5% and INDPRO falls below 95.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles characterized by rising Fed funds rates and persistent inflation reduce equity risk appetite, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where both FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% and CPI exceeds 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals strong manufacturing activity that supports corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through momentum effects.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of instances where INDPRO rises > 1.5% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with rising housing starts indicates robust consumer demand that drives corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances where both PAYEMS exceeds 100k and HOUST exceeds 800k.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation (CPI) prompts central bank rate hikes that increase discount rates and reduce equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 230.
